Hosea 14 Next, a text from Hosea, written around 750 B.C., celebrated the loving mercy of the Lord, who promised to heal Israel, to love them freely, and to welcome their return. In this optimistic context the beauty of Israel is described "as the olive tree": 4 I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him. 5 I will be as the dew unto Israel: he…
